encounter.location: Triangle Pediatrics, 4201 Lake Boone Trail, Suite 200, Raleigh, NC 27607

medications.name: prednisolone oral solution
medications.sig: complete remaining 2 days of oral prednisolone as prescribed at discharge

medications.name: epinephrine auto-injector (EpiPen Jr) 0.15 mg intramuscular injection
medications.sig: administer intramuscularly as needed for signs of anaphylaxis; carry two-pack at all times
medications.reason: peanut allergy with history of anaphylaxis

document_references.description: Documents the post-anaphylaxis follow-up phone call with mother Priya Patel, 6 days after Aisha's ED visit at WakeMed for peanut-induced anaphylaxis. Covers recovery status, EpiPen Jr training confirmation, addition of peanut allergy to allergy list, anaphylaxis event added to problem list, and referral to UNC Pediatric Allergy & Immunology. Includes counseling on strict peanut and tree nut avoidance and daycare notification recommendations.
